title: rytigynia erythroxyloides
intro: Rytigynia erythroxyloides is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine, where it is valued for its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rytigynia erythroxyloides include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with malaria, as well as its potential to treat gastrointestinal issues and wounds. Herbal preparations of Rytigynia erythroxyloides are typically made by boiling the leaves or roots in water or combining them with other medicinal plants to create a tea or infusion.
